title: ACT ON THE MANAGEMENT AND OPERATION OF 119 EMERGENCY REPORTS
text:
CHAPTER I GENERAL PROVISIONS

Article 1 (Purpose) The purpose of this Act is to prescribe matters concerning the management and operation of 119 emergency reports in fire, disasters, calamities, disasters, rescue, emergency medical services, and other emergency situations, thereby protecting personal information of reporters, etc. and strengthening their capabilities to respond to emergency reports, thereby contributing to protecting the lives, bodies, and property of citizens and improving their quality of life.

Article 2 (Definitions) The terms used in this Act are defined as follows:
  1. The term "119" means 119, which is a special number assigned in accordance with a plan for the management of telecommunications number resources under Article 48 of the Telecommunications Business Act.
  2. The term "119 emergency report" means a report filed by a person who files a report using 119 voice, text report, and other Internet, video, video, smart devices, etc. in a fire, disaster, calamity, disaster, rescue, emergency medical services, and other emergency situations (hereinafter referred to as "emergency situations").
  3. The term "agencies related to 119 emergency reporting" means emergency rescue agencies, emergency rescue and relief support agencies, disaster management agencies, and disaster management supervision agencies under Article 3 of the Framework Act on the Management of Disasters and Safety.
  4. The term "joint response" means joint response by agencies related to 119 emergency reporting by sharing reported information, where necessary to minimize damage to the lives and property of citizens due to an emergency situation.

Article 3 (Responsibilities of the State, etc.) (1) The State and local governments shall lay the groundwork necessary for receiving 119 emergency reports, sharing and transferring reported information, joint response, etc.
  (2) The State and local governments shall formulate and implement policies necessary for the research and development of new technologies related to 119 emergency reports and for the improvement of convenience and efficiency of reports.

Article 4 (Rights and Obligations of People) (1) Where an emergency response is required because an emergency situation occurs or is likely to occur or is likely to occur, anyone may request the State or a local government to promptly respond to an emergency situation by using 119 emergency reports.
  (2) No person shall file a 119 emergency report for any purpose other than a report to cope with an emergency or falsely for any purpose other than a report to cope with an emergency.

Article 5 (Overall Control and Coordination of 119 Emergency Reports) The Fire Commissioner of the National Fire Agency shall exercise general control over and coordinate affairs concerning the receipt of 119 emergency reports, the sharing and transfer of reported information, the organization of fire brigades, and the request for joint response.

Article 6 (Relationship to Other Statutes) Except as otherwise expressly provided for in other Acts, this Act shall apply to 119 emergency reports.

CHAPTER IV ESTABLISHMENT AND OPERATION OF 119 INFORMATIONAL COMMUNICATIONS SYSTEM

Article 15 (Establishment and Operation of 119 Informational Communications System) (1) The Fire Commissioner of the National Fire Agency and Mayors/Do Governors shall establish and operate a 119 information and communications system (hereinafter referred to as “119 information and communications system”) necessary for promptly receiving 119 emergency reports, sharing and transferring information on reports, joint response, support for field activities, etc.
  (2) Where necessary for the efficient operation of the 119 information and communications system, the Fire Commissioner of the National Fire Agency may inspect the operation status of the 119 information and communications system operated by a City/Do pursuant to paragraph (1).
  (3) Matters necessary for the establishment, operation, maintenance, and management of the 119 information and communications system shall be prescribed by Ordinance of the Ministry of the Interior and Safety.

Article 16 (Standardization of 119 Information and Communications Systems) (1) The Fire Commissioner of the National Fire Agency shall promote the standardization of 119 information and communications systems operated by Cities/Dos.
  (2) Where the Fire Commissioner of the National Fire Agency promotes the standardization of 119 information and communications systems operated by Cities/Dos pursuant to paragraph (1), he or she shall examine the following matters:
  1. Matters concerning the application and improvement of new technology related to 119 information and communications systems;
  2. Matters concerning the establishment and implementation of a plan for the continuity of business of the 119 information and communications system;
  3. Matters concerning the standardization, standardization, and integrated operability of the 119 information and communications system;
  4. Matters concerning interoperability between systems and ease of installation and expansion;
  5.Other matters deemed necessary by the fire commissioner of the national fire agency to be standardized.

Article 17 (Establishment of Fire-Fighting Communications Network) (1) The Fire Commissioner of the National Fire Agency and a Mayor/Do Governor shall establish and operate a firefighting communications network (referring to a communications network established to transmit and receive information, such as symbols, signals, words, videos, sound, etc., using wired or wireless lines) so that it can be utilized without delay at the scene of dispatch in the event of an emergency.
  (2) The Fire Commissioner of the National Fire Agency and a Mayor/Do Governor shall establish and operate an emergency communications network (referring to a communications network used as an emergency means when it is impossible to use ordinary means of communications where it is impossible to use ordinary means of communications due to damage to communications infrastructure, such as base stations), so as to be able to utilize the firefighting communications network in operation due to a large disaster, etc.
  (3) Matters necessary for the establishment and operation of the fire-fighting communications network under paragraph (1) and the emergency communications network under paragraph (2) shall be prescribed by Ordinance of the Ministry of the Interior and Safety.

Article 18 (Operation of Radio Frequencies for Fire Service Duties) (1) The Fire Commissioner of the National Fire Agency shall efficiently operate the radio frequencies of radio communications allocated for firefighting services pursuant to Article 9 (1) 1 of the Radio Waves Act.
  (2) Matters necessary for the operation of frequency radio frequencies for fire service duties under paragraph (1) shall be prescribed by Ordinance of the Ministry of the Interior and Safety.

Article 19 (Connection of Information Related to 119 Emergency Reports) (1) The Fire Commissioner of the National Fire Agency and a Mayor/Do Governor may request institutions related to 119 emergency response to establish a linkage system, if necessary for jointly responding to and cooperating in 119 emergency response related to 119 emergency reports. In such cases, he or she shall consult with the heads of the relevant institutions in advance.
  (2) Matters necessary for the establishment of the linkage system under paragraph (1) shall be prescribed by Presidential Decree.

Article 20 (Evaluation and Improvement of 119 Information and Communications Systems) (1) The Fire Commissioner of the National Fire Agency shall formulate and implement an evaluation plan for the operation and management of the 119 information and communications system every year for the efficient operation of the 119 information and communications system.
  (2) The Fire Commissioner of the National Fire Agency shall conduct the evaluation under paragraph (1) and notify Mayors/Do Governors of the results thereof. In such cases, the Mayors/Do Governors shall formulate and implement a plan to improve the operation and management of the 119 information and communications system by reflecting the results of the evaluation notified.
  (3) Matters concerning the evaluation of the operation and management of the 119 information and communications system shall be prescribed by Ordinance of the Ministry of the Interior and Safety.

CHAPTER Ⅴ SUPPLEMENTARY PROVISIONS

Article 21 (Securing and Support of Budget) (1) The Fire Commissioner of the National Fire Agency and Mayors/Do Governors shall endeavor to secure a budget necessary for the establishment, operation, maintenance, repair, etc. of the central and City/Do 119 information and communications systems.
  (2) The State may partially subsidize Cities/Dos for expenses incurred in establishing, operating, maintaining and repairing 119 information and communications systems under Article 15 and standardizing 119 information and communications systems under Article 16.

Article 22 (Comprehensive Management of Statistics and Information) The Fire Commissioner of the National Fire Agency shall comprehensively collect, store, analyze, analyze, process, and systematically manage information and statistics linked to the 119 information and communications system for the efficient operation of 119 emergency reports.

Article 23 (Prior Consultation) Where a central administrative agency or local government develops or introduces new technology necessary for 119 emergency reporting, it shall consult with the Fire Commissioner of the National Fire Agency in advance.

Article 24 (Education and Training) (1) The Fire Commissioner of the National Fire Agency shall prepare and distribute educational and training programs necessary for nurturing professional human resources and improving technology in connection with the operation of the 119 information and communications system.
  (2) Matters necessary for the preparation and dissemination of educational and training programs under paragraph (1) shall be determined by the Fire Commissioner of the National Fire Agency.

Article 25 (Public Relations Activities for Citizens) The Fire Commissioner of the National Fire Agency, etc. shall, if necessary for improving the convenience and convenient use of 119 emergency response services, conduct public relations activities to the people.

CHAPTER VI PENALTY PROVISIONS

Article 26 (Penalty Provisions) A person who fails to comply with a request made by the Fire Commissioner of the National Fire Agency, etc. under Article 11 (2) without good cause shall be punished by imprisonment with labor for not more than one year or by a fine not exceeding 10 million won.

Article 27 (Administrative Fines) (1) A person who files a false report on an emergency situation in violation of Article 4 (2) shall be subject to an administrative fine not exceeding five million won.
  (2) An administrative fine referred to in paragraph (1) shall be imposed and collected by the Fire Commissioner of the National Fire Agency, as prescribed by Presidential Decree.

ADDENDA <Presidential Decree No. 19872, Jan. 2, 2024>
This Act shall enter into force six months after the date of its promulgation.

title: ACT ON THE FOSTERING OF  SELF-EMPLOYED CREATIVE ENTERPRISES
text:
Article 1 (Purpose) The purpose of this Act is to contribute to the growth of the national economy by fostering self-employed creative enterprises through the promotion of the establishment of self-employed creative enterprises by the people who have creativity and specialty and through the creation of the foundation for the growth thereof.

Article 2 (Definitions) The terms used in this Act are defined as follows: <Amended on Mar. 22, 2013; Feb. 3, 2015; Jun. 10, 2022>
  1. The term "self-employed creative enterprise" means one person or a joint business entity comprised of less than five people that has creativity and expertise, who conducts business without employing regular workers (excluding a person who operates a type of business prescribed by Presidential Decree, such as real estate business);
  2. The "young self-employed creative enterprise" means a self-employed creative enterprise owned or managed by a young person aged 39 years or under that meets the standards prescribed by Presidential Decree.

Article 3 (Special Cases concerning Recognition of Self-Employed Creative Enterprises) Where a self-employed creative enterprise no longer remains a self-employed creative enterprise by reason of the expansion of its size, notwithstanding Article 2, it shall be deemed a self-employed creative enterprise for three years from the year following the year when such reason arises: Provided, That this shall not apply where it merges with an enterprise other than a self-employed creative enterprise or no longer remains a self-employed creative enterprise for other grounds prescribed by Presidential Decree.

Article 4 (Relationship to Other Acts) Except as otherwise provided for in other Acts, this Act shall apply to the fostering of self-employed creative enterprises.

Article 5 (Formulation, etc. of Plans for Fostering Self-Employed Creative Enterprises) (1) The Minister of SMEs and Startups shall formulate and execute a plan for the fostering of self-employed creative enterprises (hereinafter referred to as "plan for fostering") every three years in consultation with the heads of the central administrative agencies concerned, such as the Minister of Culture, Sports and Tourism, etc. to foster self-employed creative enterprises. <Amended on Jul. 26, 2017>
  (2) A plan for fostering shall include the following matters: <Amended on Jun. 10, 2022>
  1. Basic direction-setting for policies for the fostering of self-employed creative enterprises;
  2. Matters concerning support of startup of self-employed creative enterprises;
  3. Matters concerning creating the foundation for self-employed creative enterprises, young self-employed creative enterprises, etc.;
  4. Matters concerning statistical surveys and management related to self-employed creative enterprises;
  5. Other matters necessary for the fostering of self-employed creative enterprises.
  (3) If necessary for the formulation and execution of a plan for fostering, the Minister of SMEs and Startups may request the heads of the central administrative agencies concerned and institutions or organizations related to the fostering of self-employed creative enterprises to submit materials or state opinions. In such cases, the heads of the central administrative agencies concerned, etc. requested to submit materials or state their opinions shall comply therewith, unless extenuating circumstances exist. <Amended on Jul. 26, 2017>

Article 6 (Research on Actual Conditions) (1) In order to foster self-employed creative enterprises systematically and formulate and promote a plan for fostering efficiently, the Minister of SMEs and Startups shall conduct research on the current status of activities and the actual conditions of self-employed creative enterprises every year and publish the result thereof. <Amended on Jul. 26, 2017>
  (2) If necessary to conduct research on the actual conditions under paragraph (1), the Minister of SMEs and Startups may request public institutions under the Act on the Management of Public Institutions, self-employed creative enterprises, or related organizations to submit materials or state their opinions. In such cases, public institutions, etc. requested to submit materials or state opinions shall comply therewith, unless extenuating circumstances exist. <Amended on Jul. 26, 2017>

Article 7 (Establishment and Operation of Integrated Management System) The Minister of SMEs and Startups may establish and operate an integrated management system to comprehensively manage information related to self-employed creative enterprises, to establish the foundation for cooperation among self-employed creative enterprises, and to provide information useful to activities of self-employed creative enterprises. <Amended on Jul. 26, 2017>

Article 8 (Designation, etc. of Support Centers for Self-Employed Creative Enterprises) (1) The Government may designate an institution or organization which has specialized human resources and facilities necessary to support self-employed creative enterprises and those who intend to start a self-employed creative enterprise as a support center for self-employed creative enterprises (hereinafter referred to as "support center").
  (2) A support center shall perform the following services: <Amended on Jul. 26, 2017>
  1. Provision of workspace and conference rooms to self-employed creative enterprises;
  2. Consultation with self-employed creative enterprises about management, legal affairs, taxation matters, etc.;
  3. Other services entrusted by the Minister of SMEs and Startups.
  (3) The Government may bear all or some of expenses incurred in the performance of services under the subparagraphs of paragraph (2) to support centers designated pursuant to paragraph (1) within the budget.
  (4) Where a support center falls under any of the following, the Government may cancel its designation or suspend its business affairs wholly or partially for a fixed period not exceeding six months: Provided, That where it falls under subparagraph 1 or 1-2, the Government shall cancel its designation: <Amended on Feb. 27, 2024>
  1. Where it is designated by fraud or other improper means;
  1-2. Where it conducts business affairs during the period of suspension after it was subjected to the disposition of business suspension;
  2. Where it conducts business affairs in violation of matters designated;
  3. Where it no longer meets the standards for designation under paragraph (6).
  (5) A support center, the designation of which is cancelled pursuant to paragraph (4), shall not be re-designated within 2 years from the date of such cancellation. <Newly Inserted on Feb. 27, 2024>
  (6) Matters necessary for designation, standards and procedures for the cancellation of designation, and the operation of a support center shall be prescribed by Presidential Decree. <Amended on Feb. 27, 2024>

Article 9 (Support for Transactions of Knowledge Services) (1) The Minister of SMEs and Startups may conduct business to support self-employed creative enterprises that provide knowledge services, those who are provided with knowledge services by self-employed creative enterprises, etc. to facilitate transactions of knowledge services between self-employed creative enterprises. <Amended on Mar. 22, 2013; Jul. 26, 2017>
  (2) Necessary matters concerning business eligible for support, methods, etc. of support under paragraph (1) shall be prescribed by Presidential Decree.

Article 10 (Support for Education and Training) (1) The Government may support education and training in strengthening the specialty and competence of self-employed creative enterprises and those who intend to start a self-employed creative enterprise.
  (2) The Government may entrust a government-designated institution or organization, which is a corporation that has human resources and facilities, etc. prescribed by Presidential Decree (hereinafter referred to as "educational institution") with business affairs concerning education and training under paragraph (1).
  (3) The Government may bear all or some of expenses incurred in conducting business by educational institutions entrusted with business affairs concerning education and training pursuant to paragraph (2), as prescribed by Presidential Decree.
  (4) Where an educational institution fails to conduct business affairs concerning education and training under paragraph (1) conscientiously or fails to meet the standards for designation under paragraph (5), the Government may suspend business affairs wholly or partially for a fixed period not exceeding six months.
  (5) Matters necessary for designation, standards and procedures for the cancellation of designation, etc. of an educational institution shall be prescribed by Presidential Decree.

Article 11 (Support for Technological Development) (1) The Minister of SMEs and Startups may provide the following support for self-employed creative enterprises that have an excellent idea and technological capability: <Amended on Mar. 22, 2013; Jul. 26, 2017>
  1. Single or joint technological development of self-employed creative enterprises;
  2. Joint technological development of self-employed creative enterprises in collaboration with small and medium enterprises under the Framework Act on Small and Medium Enterprises;
  3. Other matters necessary for promoting technological development of self-employed creative enterprises.
  (2) Matters necessary for procedures for and the extent of support under paragraph (1) shall be prescribed by Presidential Decree.
  [Title Amended on Mar. 22, 2013]

Article 12 (Support for Commercialization of Ideas) (1) The Government may select self-employed creative enterprises that have an idea with a high possibility of success in commercialization and support them in their commercialization of such idea.
  (2) Where a self-employed creative enterprise selected as an enterprise eligible for support in the commercialization of an idea pursuant to paragraph (1) falls under any of the following, the Government may cancel the selection thereof: Provided, That in the case of subparagraph 1, the Government shall cancel its selection: <Amended on Feb. 3, 2015>
  1. Where the self-employed creative enterprise selected has been selected by fraud or other improper means, such as stealing the idea;
  2. Where the self-employed creative enterprise selected has relinquished the commercialization of the idea;
  3. Where it is impracticable for the self-employed creative enterprise selected to obtain the result anticipated at the beginning because the commercialization is delayed due to a reason imputable to it or it is deemed incapable of accomplishing the commercialization;
  4. Where it is impossible for the self-employed creative enterprise selected to continuously perform the commercialization due to reasons, such as insolvency or the closure of its business, or it is deemed unnecessary for such enterprise to continuously perform the commercialization.
  (3) Necessary matters concerning criteria and procedures for selection under paragraph (1), procedures for the cancellation of selection under paragraph (2), and the extent of support, etc. shall be prescribed by Presidential Decree. <Amended on Feb. 3, 2015>

Article 13 (Support for Overseas Expansion) In order to promote the expansion of self-employed creative enterprises into overseas markets, the Government may support activities, such as the international exchange of related technologies and human resources, participation in international events, etc.

Article 14 (Public Relations, etc.) The Government may push for the following activities to raise people's awareness about self-employed creative enterprises and foster self-employed creative enterprises: <Amended on Jul. 26, 2017>
  1. Finding, rewarding and publicizing successful cases of self-employed creative enterprises;
  2. Holding forums and seminars to vitalize self-employed creative enterprises;
  3. Other activities announced publicly as shall be deemed necessary by the Minister of SMEs and Startups.

Article 15 (Financial Support) (1) The Government may loan or invest necessary funds to or in self-employed creative enterprises and provide other necessary support. <Newly Inserted on Mar. 22, 2013>
  (2) The Government may have the Credit Guarantee Fund under the Credit Guarantee Fund Act, the Korea Technology Finance Corporation under the Korea Technology Finance Corporation Act, and credit guarantee foundations established pursuant to Article 9 of the Regional Credit Guarantee Foundation Act establish and operate a guarantee system intended for self-employed creative enterprises to facilitate financing for the establishment of self-employed creative enterprises and their activities. <Amended on Mar. 22, 2013; Mar. 29, 2016>
  [Title Amended on Mar. 22, 2013]

Article 15-2 (Preferential Treatment for Young Self-Employed Creative Enterprises) The Government may give preferential treatment to young self-employed creative enterprises with regard to the matters prescribed in Articles 9 through 15.
  [This Article Newly Inserted on Jun. 10, 2022]

Article 16 (Designation, etc. of Institutions Solely in Charge) (1) The Minister of SMEs and Startups may designate an institution solely in charge of business affairs on self-employed creative enterprises (hereinafter referred to as "institution solely in charge") to effectively perform policies on the fostering of self-employed creative enterprises. <Amended on Jul. 26, 2017>
  (2) The Government may bear some of the expenses incurred in the operation of an institution solely in charge within the budget.
  (3) No institutions, other than institutions solely in charge that are designated pursuant to paragraph (1), shall use any similar name.
  (4) Where an institution solely in charge designated under paragraph (1) falls under any of the following cases, the Minister of SMEs and Startups may cancel the designation: provided, in cases falling under subparagraph 1, the designation shall be cancelled: <Newly Inserted on Feb. 27, 2024>
  1. Where it has been designated as an institution solely in charge by fraud or other improper means;
  2. Where it no longer meets the standards for designation under paragraph (6).
  (5) An institution solely in charge, the designation of which is cancelled pursuant to paragraph (4), shall not be re-designated within 2 years from the date of such cancellation. <Newly Inserted on Feb. 27, 2024>
  (6) Matters necessary for the designation, operation, etc. of an institution solely in charge shall be prescribed by Presidential Decree. <Amended on Feb. 27, 2024>

Article 17 (Special Cases concerning Taxes) The State and a local government may grant tax reductions or exemptions to self-employed creative enterprises, including income tax, corporate tax, acquisition tax, property tax and registration and license tax, as prescribed by the Restriction of Special Taxation Act, the Restriction of Special Local Taxation Act and other Acts related to taxes, in order to foster self-employed creative enterprises.

Article 18 (Special Cases concerning the Food Industry Promotion Act and the Seafood Industry Promotion and Support Act) Notwithstanding Article 22 of the Food Industry Promotion Act and Article 29 of the Seafood Industry Promotion and Support Act, relaxed standards for quality certification of traditional food and traditional seafood may be otherwise prescribed regarding self-employed creative enterprises manufacturing traditional food under subparagraph 4 of Article 2 of the Food Industry Promotion Act or traditional seafood under subparagraph 7 of Article 2 of the Seafood Industry Promotion and Support Act, as prescribed by Presidential Decree. <Amended on Feb. 18, 2020>
  [Title Amended on Feb. 18, 2020]

Article 19 (Report and Examination) (1) Where the Government deems it necessary for supervision, it may request a support center to make a report or submit materials on its business affairs and property, or have a public official belonging thereto take necessary measures, such as causing him/her to access the actual site or examine documents, etc.
  (2) Where the Minister of SMEs and Startups deems it necessary for supervision, he/she may request an institution solely in charge to make a report or submit materials on its business affairs and property, or have a public official under his/her jurisdiction take necessary measures, such as causing him/her to access the actual site or examine documents, etc. <Amended on Jul. 26, 2017>
  (3) A person who has access or makes an examination pursuant to paragraphs (1) and (2) shall carry an identification card showing his/her authority and present it to interested persons.

Article 20 (Hearings) Where the Government intends to make any of the following dispositions, it shall hold a hearing: <Amended on Feb. 27, 2024>
  1. The cancellation of designation and the suspension of business affairs of a support center under Article 8 (4);
  2. The cancellation of designation and the suspension of business affairs of an educational institution under Article 10 (4);
  3. The cancellation of selection of business eligible for support in the commercialization of an idea of a self-employed creative enterprise under Article 12 (2);
  4. The cancellation of designation of an institution solely in charge under Article 16 (4).

Article 21 (Delegation or Entrustment of Authority, etc.) (1) Part of the authority of the head of a central administrative agency vested under this Act may be delegated to the Special Metropolitan City Mayor, a Metropolitan City Mayor, a Special Self-Governing City Mayor, a Do Governor, the Governor of a Special Self-Governing Province, or the head of a Si/Gun/Gu (the head of a Gu means the head of an autonomous Gu), as prescribed by Presidential Decree. <Amended on Feb. 3, 2015>
  (2) The head of a central administrative agency may entrust part of his/her business affairs under this Act to an institution solely in charge, etc., as prescribed by Presidential Decree.

Article 22 (Legal Fiction as Public Official in Application of Penalty Provisions) Executive officers and employees of an institution solely in charge, etc. engaged in business affairs entrusted by the head of a central administrative agency pursuant to Article 21 shall be deemed public officials when the penalty provisions under Articles 129 through 132 of the Criminal Act are applied.

Article 23 (Administrative Fines) (1) An administrative fine not exceeding one million won shall be imposed on a person who uses any similar name, in violation of Article 16 (3).
  (2) The Minister of SMEs and Startups shall impose and collect administrative fines under paragraph (1). <Amended on Jul. 26, 2017>

ADDENDA <Act No. 10531, Apr. 4, 2011>
Article 1 (Enforcement Date)
This Act shall enter into force six months after the date of its promulgation.
Article 2 (Transitional Measures)
(1) An institution or organization which conducts business affairs of a support center for self-employed creative enterprises after being entrusted therewith by the SME Minister of the SMBA as at the time this Act enters into force shall be deemed a support center for self-employed creative enterprises designated under this Act.
(2) An institution or organization which conducts business affairs concerning education and training after being entrusted therewith by the SME Minister of the SMBA as at the time this Act enters into force shall be deemed an educational institution designated according to this Act.

Article 1 (Enforcement Date)
(1) This Act shall enter into force on the date of its promulgation: Provided, That the amendments to the Acts to be amended pursuant to Article 5 of the Addenda, which were promulgated before this Act enters into force but the enforcement dates of which have yet to arrive, shall enter into force on the enforcement date of the relevant Act.

Article 2 (Applicability to Restriction on Designation of Support Center)

title: SPECIAL ACT ON SUPPORT FOR THE 2018 PYEONGCHANG OLYMPIC AND PARALYMPIC WINTER GAMES
SPECIAL ACT ON SUPPORT FOR  GYEONGJU ASIA-PACIFIC ECONOMIC COOPERATION 2025
text:
Article 1 (Purpose) The purpose of this Act is to contribute to the prosperity and development of the Asia-Pacific region by supporting the successful hosting of the 32nd Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation (APEC) held in Gyeongju, Gyeongsangbuk-do in 2025.

Article 2 (Definitions) The terms used in this Act are defined as follows:
  1. The term "The APEC Economic Leaders' Meeting (AELM) and other meetings" means all meetings prescribed by Presidential Decree, such as summit meetings to be held during the 32nd Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation (APEC) in 2025 and the Senior Officials' Meeting and sectoral Ministerial meetings by field to be held during the period of Republic of Korea‘s chairmanship of the APEC.
  2. The term "facilities related to AELM and other meetings" means conference halls used for the AELM and other meetings, convenience facilities and lodging facilities incidental to the conference hall, such as access roads, surrounding parks, parking lots, and other facilities prescribed by Presidential Decree, which are related to AELM and other meetings

Article 3 (Relationship to other statutes) Matters applicable to projects concerning the establishment and use of facilities related to AELM and other meetings in this Act shall prevail over other statutes.

Article 4 (Responsibilities of the State and local governments) (1) The State and local governments shall formulate and implement comprehensive policies to ensure successful hosting of AELM and other meetings.
  (2) The State and local governments shall endeavor to establish, expand, or improve lodging facilities, transportation facilities, tourism convenience facilities, etc. to be used by participants in AELM and other meetings.

Article 5 (Korea APEC 2025 Organizing Committee) (1) Korea APEC 2025 Organizing Committee (hereinafter referred to as the "Organizing Committee") shall be established under the jurisdiction of the Prime Minister to deliberate on action plans, etc. related to the hosting of the AELM and other meetings
  (2) The Organizing Committee shall deliberate on the following matters:
  1. Matters concerning plans for preparing and hosting AELM and other meetings;
  2. Matters concerning the inspection and evaluation of affairs with regards to preparations for hosting AELM and other meetings;
  3. Matters concerning the sharing of duties among administrative agencies related to AELM and other meetings;
  4. Other matters deemed necessary by the chairperson of the Organizing Committee in connection with hosting summit meetings.
  (3) The Organizing Committee shall be comprised of not more than 40 members, including one chairperson, and the Prime Minister shall serve as the chairperson of the Organizing Committee, and the following persons shall serve as its members: <Amended on Oct. 1, 2025>
  1. The Minister of Finance and Economy, the Minister of Science and ICT, the Minister of Education, the Minister of Foreign Affairs, the Minister of Justice, the Minister of the Interior and Safety, the Minister of Culture, Sports and Tourism, the Minister of Agriculture, Food and Rural Affairs, the Minister of Trade, Industry and Resources, the Minister of Health and Welfare, the Minister of Environment, the Minister of Employment and Labor, the Minister of Gender Equality and Family, the Minister of Land, Infrastructure and Transport, the Minister of Oceans and Fisheries, the Minister of SMEs and Startups, the Presidential Security Service, the Minister of the Office for Government Policy Coordination, the Minister of Food and Drug Safety, the Administrator of the Korea Disease Control and Prevention Agency, the Chariperson of the Korea Communications Commission, the Chairperson of the Fair Trade Commission, the Chairperson of the Anti-Corruption and Civil Rights Commission, and the Chairperson of the Personal Information Protection Commission;
  2. The heads of local governments having jurisdiction over AELM and other meetings;
  3. The Chairpersons of the Korea Chamber of Commerce and Industry established under the Chambers of Commerce and Industry Act, the Korea Federation of Small and Medium Business established under Article 3 (1) 4 of the Small and Medium Enterprise Cooperatives Act, the Federation of Middle Market Enterprises of Korea established under Article 27 of the Special Act on the Promotion of Growth and the Strengthening of Competitiveness of Middle-Standing Enterprises, and the Federation of Korean Industries, the Korea Enterprises Federation, and the Korea International Trade Association established under Article 32 of the Civil Act;
  4. Other personnel of institutions and private experts related to holding AELM and other meetings, commissioned by the chairperson of the Organizing Committee.
  (4) The Organizing Committee shall have one executive secretary to review matters instructed by the chairperson of the Organizing Committee and to take charge of the operation of the Organizing Committee, and the executive secretary shall be the head of the Preparatory Office for APEC 2025.
  [Enforcement date: Jan. 2, 2026]

Article 6 (Preparatory Office for APEC 2025) (1) Preparatory Office for APEC 2025 (hereinafter referred to as "Preparatory Office") shall be established under the Organizing Committee to efficiently conduct affairs necessary for the preparation and hosting of AELM and other meetings.
  (2) The Preparatory Office shall perform the following duties:
  1. Formulation and implementation of plans for the preparation and hosting AELM and other meetings;
  2. Procuring and operating human resources, equipment, facilities, etc. necessary for the preparation and hosting AELM and other meetings;
  3. Compilation and execution of budgets necessary for preparing for AELM and other meetings and operating the Preparatory Office;
  4. Coordination of affairs and cooperation with related institutions necessary for preparing for and hosting AELM and other meetings;
  5. Cooperation with local governments for preparing for and hosting AELM and other meetings;
  6. Formulation and execution of internal and external publicity plans for hosting AELM and other meetings;
  7. Support for surveys, research, data collection, etc. necessary for preparing for and hosting AELM and other meetings;
  8. Follow-up arrangement, evaluation, and reporting of AELM and other meetings;
  9. Other matters necessary for preparing for and hosting AELM and other meetings.
  (3) Other matters necessary for the organization, operation, etc. of the Preparatory Office shall be prescribed by Presidential Decree.

Article 7 (Support from the State) (1) The Preparatory Office or a local government hosting AELM and other meetings may request the State, local governments, public institutions (referring to public institutions under Article 4 of the Act on the Management of Public Institutions; hereinafter the same shall apply), corporations, organizations, etc. to provide administrative and financial cooperation and support and other necessary convenience. Upon receipt of such request, the relevant institutions shall comply therewith, except in extenuating circumstances.
  (2) The State or a local government may subsidize budgets necessary for the following matters:
  1. Development of infrastructure projects for AELM and other meetings;
  2. Creating a cityscape neighboring venues of AELM and other meetings;
  3. Construction, renovation, and maintenance of facilities related to AELM and other meetings;
  4. Other matters necessary for hosting AELM and other meetings.
  (3) The State or local governments may preferentially implement projects for facilities related to AELM and other meetings, in accordance with the timing of hosting AELM and other meetings, as prescribed by Presidential Decree.
  (4) The heads of the State, local governments, and relevant administrative agencies may support volunteer activities for the successful hosting of AELM and other meetings pursuant to plans for AELM and other meetings.

Article 8 (Receipt of donations) (1) Notwithstanding Article 5 (2) of the Act on Collection and Use of Donations and Promotion of Donation Culture, the Preparatory Office may receive donations voluntarily deposited within the scope consistent with the purpose of the project.
  (2) Matters necessary for the procedures for and method of receiving donations under paragraph (1) shall be prescribed by Presidential Decree.

Article 9 (Lending of the State and public property) (1) Where the State or a local government deems it necessary to support the operation and activities of the Preparatory Office or a local government hosting AELM and other meetings, notwithstanding the State Property Act, the Commodity Management Act, and the Public Property and Commodity Management Act, the State or the local government may lend State or public property gratuitously to the extent that it does not interfere with its use, or allow it to use State or public property gratuitously or to transfer office supplies or other commodities or to use such goods gratuitously.
  (2) Where the Minister of Land, Infrastructure and Transport gratuitously lends, allows the use of property or commodities, or permits or transfers profits pursuant to paragraph (1), the details, conditions, procedures, etc. thereof shall be determined by a contract between the managing authority of the relevant property or commodities and the Preparatory Office, or the local government hosting AELM and other meetings.

Article 10 (Issuance of commemorative coins) The Preparatory Office or local governments hosting AELM and other meetings may request the Bank of Korea to issue commemorative coins to publicize or commemorate AELM and other meetings.

Article 11 (Issuance of commemorative stamps) The Preparatory Office or local governments hosting AELM and other meetings may request the Minister of Science and ICT to issue commemorative postage stamps or postcards to publicize or commemorate AELM and other meetings.

Article 12 (Request for dispatch of public officials) The Preparatory Office may request the heads of relevant administrative agencies or related institutions or organizations to dispatch public officials, executive officers or employees under their control, if necessary to perform their duties.

Article 13 (Requests for provision of data) (1) The Preparatory Office may request the relevant administrative agencies, public institutions, educational institutions, research organizations, etc. to provide data, such as investigation reports, research papers, etc. related to summit meetings.
  (2) The head of an institution or organization in receipt of a request under paragraph (1) shall comply therewith, unless there is a compelling reason not to do so.

Article 14 (Formulation of measures for special transportation and lodging) (1) The heads of local governments hosting AELM and other meetings shall formulate special transportation and accommodation measures.
  (2) The heads of local governments hosting AELM and other meetings shall organize and operate the headquarters for special transportation and accommodation measures.
  (3) The heads of local governments hosting AELM and other meetings may request the head of a public institution to provide necessary cooperation, such as dispatching employees belonging to the headquarters for special transportation and accommodation measures , if necessary to implement special transportation and accommodation measures formulated pursuant to paragraph (1).
  (4) The State and local governments may subsidize the budget necessary for local governments hosting AELM and other meetings to formulate and implement measures for special transportation and lodging.

Article 15 (Use of insignia of AELM) Any person who intends to use an insignia designated by the Preparatory Office or any similar emblem of a summit meeting shall obtain prior approval from the Preparatory Office.

Article 16 (Prohibition of use of similar title) No one shall use the title "32nd Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation (APEC) in 2025" or any similar title without approval from the Preparatory Office.

Article 17 (Legal fiction as public official in application of penalty provisions) Executive officers and employees dispatched to the Preparatory Office from a related institution or organization pursuant to Article 12 shall be deemed public officials for the purposes of penalty provisions under Articles 127 and 129 through 132 of the Criminal Act.

Article 18 (Administrative fines) (1) A person who violates Articles 15 or 16 shall be subject to an administrative fine not exceeding 3 million won.
  (2) An administrative fine under paragraph (1) shall be imposed and collected by the Minister of Foreign Affairs, as prescribed by Presidential Decree.

ADDENDUM <Presidential Decree No. 20572, Dec. 20, 2024>
Article 1 (Enforcement date)
This Act shall enter into force on the date of its promulgation.
Article 2 (Term of validity)
This Act shall be effective until Jun. 30, 2026.

ADDENDA <Act No. 21065, Oct. 1, 2025>
Article 1 (Enforcement date)
This Act shall enter into force on the date of its promulgation; provided, part of the Act amended by the Addenda 7 to the Government Organization Act, which was promulgated before the Government Organization Act enters into force but the enforcement date of which has not arrived yet shall enter into force on the enforcement date of such Act.
1.The amended provisions of each of the following items shall enter into force on January 2, 2026; provided, the parts of laws amended under Article 7 of the Addenda (limited to the parts related to the amended provisions of items a and b) that were promulgated before the enforcement date under the main clause but whose enforcement date under the main clause has not yet arrived, the amended parts shall enter into force on the enforcement date of the relevant law;
a. Amended provisions of Articles 19 (4), 23, 29 (1) 1, and 30;
b. Amended provisions of Article 12 (2), Article 19 (3), Article 22, and the proviso to Article 29 (2) (limited to matters concerning the Minister of Finance and Economy and the Ministry of Finance and Economy);
c. Laws amended under Article 7 of the Addenda (limited to the parts related to the amended provisions of items a and b).
2. Omitted.
